wiki.content: "JRJRJR" is the first single from Jane Remover's third album Revengeseekerz, released on January 1, 2025, alongside its music video and the album announcement. The track serves as a raw exploration of Jane's complex relationship with her artistic identity, fame, and fanbase, particularly following her June 2022 name change from an alias containing her deadname. Jane expresses profound discomfort even with her current alias ("Bitch, I hate the way it rolls off the tongue, full circle"), contemplating another change and feeling trapped in a cycle related to her identity shifts. The song painfully details the experience of performing older material she dislikes and feels disconnected from ("Rehearsing songs I hate in Silverlake, trying not to cry / Then I step up off the stage and they don’t know I lost my mind"). It confronts frustrating fan interactions, including jokes about her name and the broader "irony epidemic" impacting how her vulnerable art is received, highlighting a desire to be taken more seriously. Featuring a return to rap-influenced vocals, "JRJRJR" sets a confrontational and vulnerable tone for the Revengeseekerz era, grappling with artistic evolution against the weight of past identities and audience perception. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.
